reasoning:
The Fed officially ended Quantitative Tightening (QT) on December 1, 2025, and began reinvesting maturing MBS into Treasury bills, shifting the macro trend from strict drawdown to flat/slight organic growth. 
The comparison date (2025-12-21) resolves to the 12-17 Wednesday value, which the FRED data shows is 6.557 trillion (up $22B from early December). This elevation is standard given the holiday season currency demand and year-end repo/SRF liquidity spikes. 
1. For 2025-12-28 (resolves to 12-24 data): Peak holiday liquidity needs will likely push the balance sheet even higher than 12-17, making an increase highly probable (0.68).
2. For 2026-01-20 (resolves to 01-14 data): Post-holiday, excess currency naturally returns and year-end repos roll off, which historically causes a temporary drop. The baseline organic growth since Dec 1 is unlikely to have outpaced this ~$22B holiday padding yet, pointing to a value lower than the 12-17 mark (0.38). 
3. For 2026-03-21 (resolves to 03-18 data): Over three months, the Fed's planned "modest Treasury bond buying" and continuous organic growth to track currency demand should accumulate enough baseline to surpass the Dec 17 holiday-padded watermark. However, due to the instruction that forecast uncertainty must strictly increase with time, the probability is bounded closer to 0.5 (0.55).
